spec_match.rationale: Concept frames the spec correctly: sources persisted → still capped; triggers ephemeral → uncapped. JSON snippets are well-formed and named correctly.

interactive.rationale: Live text input with preset buttons drives a real-time verdict for both source and trigger sides — character counts update live, verdict pills flip colour at the boundary.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card promises a real-time both-sides verdict as the visitor types; the page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Boundary tested but no zero-length probe, no whitespace-only key probe, no Unicode-key probe (multi-byte chars where the cap counts code units not chars). These are the realistic gotchas an ad-tech engineer will hit.

references.rationale: see also links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5131578140000256 and the WICG spec — meets rule #3.

design_system.rationale: All colours via CSS variables, panels use the design system, no static-card pattern, button preset overrides both background and color together — no light-on-light regression.

openQuestions.title: Add a UTF-8 / code-unit edge case preset
openQuestions.detail: The 25-char cap is on code units, not user-perceived characters. A preset key containing emoji or a combining accent would surface this — useful for ad-tech operators trying to support i18n campaign names. Add a preset like `campaign_🎯_q4_eu` (which is well under the limit visually but over once you count code units).

openQuestions.title: Note that the cap counts source keys ARRAY length too
openQuestions.detail: The cap on aggregation_keys also caps the NUMBER of keys per source (currently 20). The page only addresses key-name length. A small note or secondary verdict would round out the picture.

summary: Clean, honest side-by-side demo with real interactivity, working references, and design-system discipline. Just missing a couple of i18n / spec-edge details to be fully comprehensive.
